title: A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK Guide) – 7th Edition
author: Project Management Institute
summary: The definitive guide to project management, now updated with a principles-based approach that reflects modern delivery methods.

reviews.text: The 7th edition is a huge shift from prescriptive processes to principles. It finally acknowledges that not every project fits a waterfall mould. Essential reading if you're in project delivery, though it can feel abstract without real-world examples to anchor it.

title: Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI
author: Ethan Mollick
summary: Ethan Mollick explores how AI is reshaping work, creativity, and education — with practical frameworks for using it effectively.

reviews.text: One of the best books on practical AI adoption I've read. Mollick doesn't hype or fear-monger — he gives you real frameworks for integrating AI into your work. If you're in project delivery and wondering how AI fits in, start here.
Really accessible and well-written. I appreciated the focus on practical application rather than just theory. Lost a star because some sections felt a bit repetitive, but overall a must-read.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
summary: Nobel laureate Daniel Kahneman reveals the two systems that drive how we think and make decisions — essential for anyone managing projects or teams.

reviews.text: A foundational book for understanding decision-making. As a project manager, understanding cognitive biases has been one of the most valuable skills I've developed. This book is the starting point for that journey.
